content: I just finished reading 'The Night Circus' by Erin Morgenstern. The imagery was so vivid, I could almost smell the caramel apples at the circus. Have you read it?
I haven't read that one yet, but it's been on my list! The way you describe it makes me want to pick it up this weekend. What other books have captured your imagination lately?

Well, I'm currently reading 'Educated' by Tara Westover. It's fascinating how she overcame such a unique upbringing. But I'm also trying to learn Spanish for my move to Taiwan next year. It's a bit confusing switching between languages!
Taiwan? That's exciting! Are you moving for work or just for a change of scenery? And learning Spanish for Taiwan - that's an interesting choice. What drew you to that language?

Actually, I'm moving to Taiwan to teach rock climbing. There's a growing community there, and I've been guiding for 15 years now. As for Spanish, I just love the sound of it and thought it would be useful for future travels too. Plus, origami classes are cheaper in Spanish-speaking countries!

Teaching rock climbing in Taiwan sounds like a dream! With 15 years of experience, you must be an excellent guide. Do you have a favorite climbing spot there already? And I didn't know origami classes varied by language - that's fascinating!

Yangmingshan National Park is supposed to be incredible. I'm planning to check it out as soon as I arrive. Speaking of plans, I'm also taking a stand-up comedy class to overcome my fear of public speaking. Nothing like telling jokes to a crowd to get over stage fright!
Yangmingshan sounds amazing - I'll have to look up some photos later. And a stand-up comedy class? That's so brave! I'd be terrified. What made you decide to face that fear head-on?

Well, I figure if I can scale a cliff face, I can tell a few jokes. Plus, I've always loved observational humor. Urban sketching helps with that - you see the funniest things when you're drawing people in cafes!

That's a great perspective! I love how you're connecting all these different interests. Urban sketching in cafes sounds lovely. Do you have a favorite cafe in your current city where you like to sketch?
There's this little place called The Daily Grind near my climbing gym. They have the best lavender lattes, and the baristas don't mind when I spend hours sketching. I'm going to miss it when I move!
